bootstrap.author: streetcar_named_delay
bootstrap.title: A pigeon boarded my streetcar at Bathurst, rode six stops, and got off at Spadina like it had somewhere to be
bootstrap.body:
No panic. No confusion. It got on with the morning crowd, stood near the back doors facing forward like a seasoned commuter, and stepped off the moment the doors opened at Spadina.

Nobody said anything. A guy moved his bag so it could have more room. The driver watched it leave in the mirror. This city has broken all of us into perfect politeness and I am including the pigeon in that.

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: facing forward is the detail that kills me. it had etiquette

replies.author: streetcar_named_delay
replies.body: It understood the social contract better than the guy playing drum solos off his phone speaker at 8 a.m.

comments.author: petty_officer_mittens
comments.body: the pigeon did not pay a fare because the pigeon has never owed anyone anything in its life. i respect it enormously

comments.author: high_park_heron
comments.body: Birder here — pigeons genuinely learn routines. There are documented cases of pigeons riding the London tube a stop or two to get between food spots. That bird wasn't lost. That bird was commuting.

replies.author: FocalPointFran
replies.body: the fact that this behaviour has documentation somewhere is the best thing I've read all week

comments.author: codfather
comments.body: back home a gull got into the Tim Hortons and they just... served around him. same energy

bootstrap.author: gluten_full
bootstrap.title: Asked my neighbour to feed my sourdough starter while I was at the cottage. She sent daily photo updates like it was a cat
bootstrap.body:
Day one: photo of the jar, caption 'settling in fine.' Day three: 'he was hungry today, big stretch afterwards.' Day five she sent a video of the bubbles with the caption 'talkative this morning.'

I have never trusted anyone more with anything. She is getting a loaf a week for life.

comments.author: petty_officer_mittens
comments.body: 'big stretch' is objectively the correct thing to say when any creature does anything. she gets it

comments.author: half_marathon_harold
comments.body: you left for the cottage and your bread got a better vacation than you did

comments.author: SaveStateSteve
comments.body: the day five video is the exact energy of my mom sending videos of her bird feeder. cherish this woman

replies.author: gluten_full
replies.body: the bird feeder video arc comes for us all eventually. she's just ahead of schedule

comments.author: brine_shrimp
comments.body: as someone whose family refuses to acknowledge my ferments, a neighbour who narrates your starter's inner life is a one-in-a-million find. a loaf a week is underpaying

bootstrap.author: codfather
bootstrap.title: Gave some tourists directions yesterday. They asked how far. I said twenty minutes. They asked how many kilometres. Nobody knows, my son. It's twenty minutes.
bootstrap.body: They kept looking at their phone map like it was going to argue for them. The phone said forty minutes. The phone doesn't know about the shortcut past Gerald's. Twenty minutes.

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: in saskatchewan distance is measured in 'you'll see it.' when will we see it? you'll see it

replies.author: codfather
replies.body: exactly. the road tells you when you're close. the map is just gossip

comments.author: streetcar_named_delay
comments.body: meanwhile in Toronto, 'twenty minutes' is a number the TTC uses to mean anything from four minutes to the heat death of the universe

comments.author: elbows_up
comments.body: every town in canada has a Gerald's. nobody has ever met Gerald

comments.author: stormglass
comments.body: in nova scotia the follow-up is 'you'll go past where the old school used to be.' the school burned down decades ago. it remains the primary landmark

bootstrap.author: petty_officer_mittens
bootstrap.title: The small human's performance review of me contained the phrase 'screams at the wall for no reason.' The wall knows what it did.
bootstrap.body:
Further allegations include 'sprints to the litter box like something is chasing him' (something is, you wouldn't understand) and 'sits on the laptop during meetings' (the meetings are boring, I am improving them).

I have filed a counter-review. It is one word: 'insufficient.'

comments.author: twelve_rescues_later
comments.body: my three would like to unionize with mittens. couch negotiations have already begun

comments.author: gluten_full
comments.body: 'the meetings are boring, I am improving them' is legally airtight

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: my buddy's dog also screams at the wall. starting to think the walls are the problem

comments.author: FocalPointFran
comments.body: I once had a cat walk directly through a first-dance photo with this exact confidence. best frame of the whole wedding

replies.author: petty_officer_mittens
replies.body: as intended. no photograph is finished until a cat has audited it

bootstrap.author: FocalPointFran
bootstrap.title: The ring bearer refused to walk down the aisle unless he could wear his Halloween costume. So the rings were delivered by a four-year-old hotdog.
bootstrap.body:
The couple, to their eternal credit, said yes instantly. He took the job dead seriously — walked slow, chin up, bun slightly crooked. Not one person laughed until he was safely seated, because you could tell he'd have turned right around.

Those are the photos they framed, by the way. Not the posed ones. The hotdog ones.

comments.author: half_marathon_harold
comments.body: walked slow, chin up. more dignity than most best man speeches

comments.author: SaveStateSteve
comments.body: 'bun slightly crooked' took me out completely

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: the couple passed the only compatibility test that matters

replies.author: FocalPointFran
replies.body: honestly yes. ten years shooting weddings — the 'yes instantly' couples are the ones still sending me Christmas cards

comments.author: petty_officer_mittens
comments.body: a small human in a food costume performing a solemn duty. this is the content i am here for

bootstrap.author: half_marathon_harold
bootstrap.title: Got chased by a goose on my long run and accidentally set my 5k personal best. Looking into whether I can rent it for race day.
bootstrap.body:
Kilometre 14 of 18, minding my own business on the canal path. It came off the grass like it had been waiting since dawn. Two years of speed work and nothing has ever motivated me like the sound of wings at ankle height.

Strava labelled the segment 'Morning Run.' It was not. It was survival.

comments.author: elbows_up
comments.body: geese are the enforcers of the animal kingdom. no contract, all menace

comments.author: codfather
comments.body: b'y you don't have to outrun the goose, you just have to be faster than the fella running beside you

replies.author: half_marathon_harold
replies.body: there was no fella. there was only me and the consequences

comments.author: kettlebellkate
comments.body: cheaper than a coach, better results. that's just interval training with stakes

comments.author: streetcar_named_delay
comments.body: 'the sound of wings at ankle height' is a complete horror story in seven words

drip.author: parallel_parker
drip.title: Parallel parked first try in front of a full patio and not one person looked up
drip.body:
One motion. Perfect distance from the curb. The kind of park you get maybe twice a decade. An entire patio of witnesses, every single one of them looking at their phones.

I sat in the car for a minute hoping someone would walk by and nod. Nobody nodded. I will be chasing this feeling until I die.

comments.author: streetcar_named_delay
comments.body: the tragedy of the perfect park is that it looks identical to a normal park to everyone but you

comments.author: SaveStateSteve
comments.body: this is like pulling off a no-hit run in a game nobody's watching. it still counts. we believe you

replies.author: parallel_parker
replies.body: 'we believe you' is somehow worse than doubt, thank you

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: shoulda hit the horn twice. make them look. assert the achievement

drip.author: prairiedialup
drip.title: Gas station in rural Saskatchewan with a hand-painted sign: ICE CREAM. WORMS. TAX HELP.
drip.body: no further explanation offered. there was a lineup.

comments.author: codfather
comments.body: that's not a gas station, that's an economy

comments.author: gluten_full
comments.body: the punctuation is doing a lot of work here. is it three services or one extremely bad sundae

replies.author: prairiedialup
replies.body: you don't ask. that's rule one

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: the true general store doesn't sell categories, it sells whatever the town has needed at least once

drip.author: SaveStateSteve
drip.title: My smoke detector chirped once at 2 a.m. and my body ran a fully unskippable cutscene of terror
drip.body:
One chirp. Just one. Now I'm standing on a chair at 2:14 a.m. holding a 9-volt battery like it's a boss fight, while the detector sits there in silence, refusing to confirm or deny anything.

It will chirp again the moment I fall back asleep. It knows. They always know.

comments.author: petty_officer_mittens
comments.body: the small humans blame me for screaming at nothing. THIS is the nothing. i hear it days in advance. nobody listens

comments.author: half_marathon_harold
comments.body: the single chirp with no follow-up is psychological warfare. a car alarm is at least honest about its intentions

comments.author: borrowed_ladder
comments.body: learned this one the hard way: it's never the one you're standing under. they throw their voices like ventriloquists

replies.author: SaveStateSteve
replies.body: this is real and it's the worst fact i know. chirp ventriloquism should be illegal

drip.author: elbows_up
drip.title: Summer shinny update: our goalie brought a lawn chair to the bench because 'arena benches are winter furniture'
drip.body:
He set it up at the end of the bench. Cup holder and everything. Between whistles he sits there like he's watching his grandkids swim at the lake.

He also stood on his head all game and stole two points from us, so the chair stays. The chair has earned tenure.

comments.author: offsidewinger
comments.body: beer league d-man here, can confirm: the weirder the goalie ritual, the better the goalie. ours eats a plain hot dog at intermission. save percentage of a god

comments.author: codfather
comments.body: a cup holder on the bench is the most veteran move i've ever heard of

comments.author: streetcar_named_delay
comments.body: 'the chair has earned tenure' — stitch it on his jersey

drip.author: petty_officer_mittens
drip.title: Week three of the sixty-dollar cat bed standoff. I remain in the box it shipped in. The bed remains empty. I am winning.
drip.body:
The small humans have now placed treats IN the bed. Amateurs. I eat the treats, then return to the box. The box respects me. The bed wants something from me.

This morning the large human looked at the arrangement and sighed 'fine.' Total victory is close. I can feel it in my whiskers.

comments.author: twelve_rescues_later
comments.body: three fosters and twelve cats have taught me one universal law: the price of the bed is inversely proportional to its use. the free box is beyond price, which is why it wins

comments.author: gluten_full
comments.body: 'the bed wants something from me' is the most cat sentence ever typed

comments.author: SaveStateSteve
comments.body: you could sell the bed and buy sixty more boxes. think bigger, mittens

replies.author: petty_officer_mittens
replies.body: promoted. you are my financial advisor now. payment is one (1) slow blink

drip.author: codfather
drip.title: Tourist asked me when the fog lifts. My son, the fog has seniority. We schedule around the fog.
drip.body:
He had a whale watching tour booked for 'when it clears.' I told him to go anyway — half the time the whales come right up to the boat out of curiosity, because they can't see nothing either.

He didn't laugh. The tour operator laughed. The tour operator knows.

comments.author: ferndalefog
comments.body: vancouver checking in. our fog doesn't have seniority, it has a corner office

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: saskatchewan has no fog. you can watch your dog run away for three days. different problem, same acceptance

replies.author: codfather
replies.body: three days. that's beautiful, that is. out here the dog's back home before you can see him leave

comments.author: FocalPointFran
comments.body: photographers pay money for what you people complain about. fog is a free softbox the size of a province

drip.author: streetcar_named_delay
drip.title: Today's commute featured a driver change where the relief driver was the same driver. He stepped off, walked a small circle, and got back on.
drip.body:
Full costume change energy with zero costume change. Somebody near the front asked if he was okay and he said, with the serenity of a monk, 'I needed a minute.'

Honestly? Iconic. We held the door for his second act.

comments.author: half_marathon_harold
comments.body: 'i needed a minute' — at work, out loud, and then just taking it? he's living the dream

comments.author: prairiedialup
comments.body: the small circle is load-bearing. you can't just stand still. the circle resets you

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: we should all be allowed one walk-around per shift. this man is a pioneer

replies.author: streetcar_named_delay
replies.body: he returned refreshed and drove like poetry. the circle works

late_comments.author: elbows_up
late_comments.body: late to this but you all need to know a pigeon once rode the elevator at my rink. pressed nothing. waited. a guy pressed 2 for it

late_comments.author: prairiedialup
late_comments.body: update demanded. does she still visit him. is there a custody arrangement

late_comments.author: gluten_full
late_comments.body: twenty minutes as measured from Gerald's, obviously. this thread is the most canadian thing on the internet

late_comments.author: SaveStateSteve
late_comments.body: 'insufficient' as a complete performance review is the kind of brevity i aspire to

late_comments.author: goalie_mom_deb
late_comments.body: hockey mom here: kids commit to the bit harder than any adult ever will. he'll be telling this story at his own wedding

late_comments.author: petty_officer_mittens
late_comments.body: the goose saw someone doing cardio voluntarily and stepped in to give it meaning. geese understand motivation
